title: Governance First
description: Define action boundaries, permissions, and human checkpoints before anything runs autonomously.
implementation: 4 human approval gates in chat flow, audit trail in SQLite.
subRules: Map every autonomous action to a risk level (low/medium/high/critical) before deployment
Require explicit human approval for any action involving financial transactions over $100
Maintain immutable audit logs for every autonomous decision with timestamp, context, and outcome
Review and update governance boundaries monthly as the system learns and scales

title: Zero-Waste Mindset
description: Zero waste, zero defects, zero downtime.
implementation: Validation loops at every stage, error handling, health monitoring.
subRules: Every pipeline stage must validate its output before passing to the next stage
Implement automatic retry with exponential backoff for transient failures
Monitor uptime and set alerts for any service degradation above 0.1% error rate
Track resource utilization and eliminate idle compute, unused APIs, and redundant processes

title: Agentic Constitution
description: Every autonomous business needs a constitution defining what AI can and cannot do.
subRules: Document explicit capability boundaries for each AI agent in a machine-readable format
Define escalation paths for edge cases the AI cannot handle autonomously
Publish the constitution alongside the business so stakeholders understand AI limits
Version-control the constitution and require human sign-off on any changes to AI permissions

title: Human-in-the-Loop
description: Autonomous for routine, human approval for high-stakes/irreversible actions.
implementation: ApprovalGate component, all deployments require human click.
source: Industry best practice for AI safety
subRules: Classify every action as routine (auto-approve), notable (notify), or critical (block until approved)
Implement an ApprovalGate UI component that clearly shows what the AI wants to do and why
Set timeout policies — if no human responds within 24 hours, escalate rather than auto-approve
Log every human override decision to improve the autonomy model over time
Never allow AI to modify its own approval thresholds without human consent

title: Build Systems Not Tasks
description: Build systems, not tasks — own AI systems for passive income.
implementation: Each business is a deployable system with its own lifecycle.
subRules: Design every business as a self-contained system with inputs, processing, and outputs
Automate the entire lifecycle: discovery, creation, deployment, monitoring, and iteration
Favor recurring revenue models that generate income without per-transaction human effort
Build composable components that can be reused across multiple business systems

title: Validation Loops
description: Every autonomous action must be verifiable and auditable.
implementation: Execution logs, checkpoint history, automated test suites.
subRules: Run automated validation after every pipeline stage before proceeding
Store checkpoint snapshots so any stage can be rolled back and replayed
Implement health checks that run continuously, not just at deployment time
Use structured logging with correlation IDs to trace any action end-to-end

title: Progressive Autonomy
description: Start manual, automate incrementally, validate at each level.
implementation: Phased deployment from manual to semi-auto to fully autonomous.
subRules: Launch every new business in manual mode first — human does everything, AI observes
Graduate to semi-autonomous only after 10+ successful manual cycles with zero critical errors
Move to full autonomy only for actions with a proven track record of >99% success rate
Maintain a kill switch that instantly reverts any process to manual mode

title: Multi-Revenue Streams
description: Don't depend on one income source.
implementation: Pipeline supports multiple concurrent businesses with diversified revenue.
source: Portfolio theory applied to autonomous business
subRules: Run at least 3 businesses across different categories to reduce single-point-of-failure risk
Balance high-risk/high-reward ventures with stable recurring-revenue businesses
Track revenue correlation — if two businesses depend on the same platform, count them as one risk
Reinvest 20% of profits into launching new revenue streams quarterly

title: API-First Architecture
implementation: All features exposed as API routes, webhook-driven integrations.
subRules: Expose every business capability as a documented REST or GraphQL API endpoint
Use webhooks for event-driven communication between services — never poll when you can push
Version all APIs from day one to allow safe iteration without breaking integrations
Design for composability — any two business systems should be connectable via API

title: Fractal Organization
description: Each business unit mirrors the whole, operates independently.
implementation: Self-contained units with own monitoring, deployment, and revenue tracking.
subRules: Each business unit must have its own health dashboard, revenue tracking, and alerting
Design units to be deployable independently — no shared databases or tightly coupled services
Apply the same governance, validation, and autonomy rules at every level of the organization
Enable any single business unit to be sold, replicated, or shut down without affecting others
